title: Builders Clean in Melbourne: What's Included and When to Book
excerpt: Everything you need to know about post-construction cleaning — what a builders clean covers, why it's different, and how to time it with your build.
htmlContent:
A builders clean is one of the most specialised types of professional cleaning — and also one of the most important. After any construction or renovation work, a standard clean simply won't cut it. Here's what a professional builders clean involves and when you need one.

Why Post-Construction Cleaning Is Different

Construction work generates a specific kind of mess that household cleaning equipment isn't designed to handle:

  • Fine construction dust that penetrates everywhere — inside cupboards, behind power points, in ventilation systems
  • Paint overspray on windows, tiles, and fixtures
  • Adhesive and silicone residue on surfaces
  • Plaster dust on floors and in corners
  • Concrete splatter on tiles and glass
  • Sawdust in carpet fibres and floor joins
  • Sticker and label residue on new windows and appliances

Each of these requires specific products and techniques. Using the wrong approach — particularly on new surfaces like fresh tiles, timber floors, or new appliances — can cause permanent damage.

What a Professional Builders Clean Includes

Initial Construction Clean

Done during the build, before final finishes are installed. Focuses on removing heavy debris so trades can work in a clean environment:

  • Removal of construction debris and waste
  • Vacuum all surfaces including walls and ceilings
  • Clean floors throughout

Final Builders Clean

The most comprehensive stage — done after all trades have finished and before handover or occupation:

  • Windows: Remove all paint overspray, concrete splatter, stickers, and construction residue from glass — inside and outside
  • Hard floors: Remove grout haze from tiles, clean timber floors with appropriate products, polish concrete where applicable
  • Kitchen: Clean inside all cabinetry, remove stickers from appliances and benchtops, clean oven and rangehood
  • Bathrooms: Remove silicone smears, grout haze, remove tap and fitting stickers, descale and polish
  • Walls and ceilings: Remove paint splatter, dust cornices and ceiling edges
  • Doors and frames: Remove adhesive, wipe all door frames and architraves
  • Light fittings: Clean covers and globes, remove any protective film
  • Exterior: Clean paths, remove concrete splatter, wipe exterior windows

When to Book a Builders Clean

Timing is critical. Book the final builders clean:

  • After all trades have completed their work — if painters or tilers come back after the clean, you'll need it redone
  • 2–3 days before handover or settlement — allows time to address anything that's been missed
  • Before occupation — once furniture is moved in, access is limited and costs increase

Coordinate with your builder to confirm a fixed "trades completed" date before booking. Ask whether any touch-up painting or snagging work will happen after the clean — if so, schedule the clean after that work is finished.

What to Look for in a Builders Cleaning Company

  • Experience with post-construction environments (different to domestic cleaning)
  • Products appropriate for new surfaces — wrong products can etch new tiles, streak fresh glass, or damage timber floors
  • Public liability insurance — essential if working on a building site
  • Ability to work with builders and developers, not just homeowners
